investments.headline: Bioliberty £7.7m investment
investments.summary: University of Edinburgh spinout Bioliberty secured £7.7m in Series A funding to accelerate product development and support international growth of its AI-enabled soft-robotic rehabilitation technology. The Scottish National Investment Bank led the round with £3m, supported by existing and new investors.
investments.raw_snippet: Edinburgh Innovations news confirms Bioliberty secured £7.7m in Series A investment to accelerate product development and international growth. The company is headquartered at The National Robotarium in Edinburgh with manufacturing in Fife. Announced 19 March 2026.

investments.headline: Earth Blox secures £6m to help businesses and banks finance the transition to a nature-positive economy
investments.summary: Edinburgh-based Earth Blox secured £6m led by PXN Ventures to expand its nature risk analytics platform, which combines satellite data with AI to help financial institutions assess ecosystem degradation impacts. The round includes follow-on investment from Archangels, Scottish Enterprise, and the European Space Agency. The funding will support team expansion and accelerate product development.
investments.raw_snippet: Earth Blox, an Edinburgh-based startup, secured £6m in new funding led by PXN Ventures, with participation from Archangels (follow-on), Scottish Enterprise, and the European Space Agency. The company's platform integrates satellite data, environmental information, and portfolio analysis using AI to help global banks, consumer brands, energy firms, and agricultural businesses assess how ecosystem degradation and climate hazards impact financial performance. A new Director of Nature and Climate from PwC UK was announced simultaneously.

investments.headline: BIOCAPTIVA secures £1.58M to transform how blood samples are prepared for liquid biopsy
investments.summary: BIOCAPTIVA, a University of Edinburgh spinout based at the Easter Bush Campus, raised £1.58m led by Archangels to advance its magnetic bead platform for liquid biopsy cancer testing. The patented msX technology extracts cell-free DNA from whole blood without centrifuges, enabling faster processing and higher-quality results. Funds will support US market entry and R&D expansion.
investments.raw_snippet: BIOCAPTIVA secured £1.58m in funding led by Archangels, with participation from Old College Capital, BBI, Scottish Enterprise, and new investor EverQuest Capital Partners. The company's msX technology extracts cell-free DNA directly from whole blood without centrifuges, enabling larger samples, faster extraction, simpler processing, and fully automatable capabilities. CEO Jeremy Wheeler said the innovation 'has the potential to revolutionise how samples are collected' for liquid biopsy cancer research.

investments.source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via ADBA press release)
investments.headline: $3 Million Investment for Edinburgh-Based Specialist Bio-Carbon Designer Carbogenics
investments.summary: Carbogenics, a University of Edinburgh spinout founded in 2016, closed a $3m funding round on January 19, 2026 to accelerate development and international expansion of its CreChar biochar product. Equity investors include Dangerous Ventures (US), Green Angel Ventures (UK), and Old College Capital; grant funding from Innovate UK, New Mexico Gas, and New Mexico Government Economic Development Department also contributed. The company plans US manufacturing expansion alongside scaled UK operations.
investments.raw_snippet: Carbogenics closed $3 million in investment and grant funding on January 19, 2026. Equity investors: Dangerous Ventures, Green Angel Ventures, Old College Capital (University of Edinburgh venture fund), Scottish Enterprise. Grant bodies: Innovate UK, New Mexico Gas, New Mexico Government Economic Development Department. Funds accelerate development of CreChar biochar for the biogas industry and support US manufacturing expansion.

investments.source_name: DuckDuckGo — Scottish VC search (via Daily Business)
investments.headline: SWURF raises £200k to expand platform
investments.summary: Edinburgh-based SWURF, a remote-working platform connecting workers to on-demand private meeting pods, raised £200k in March 2026 from existing angel backers including Skyscanner co-founder Gareth Williams, alongside new unnamed high-net-worth investors. The funding will accelerate rollout of SWURF Pods across Edinburgh with the goal of making Edinburgh the world's most flexible working-friendly city.
investments.raw_snippet: SWURF secured £200,000 of investment on March 9, 2026, to accelerate expansion. Existing backers include Gareth Williams (Skyscanner co-founder) and Anna Lagerqvist Christopherson (owner of Boda BV, Green Room and Victoria Bar). New investors are unnamed high-net-worth individuals. Platform currently lists over 450 venues and serves approximately 14,000 users. Board chaired by Alison Grieve (G-Hold founder).

investments.headline: Spinout Exergy3 raises £10m to decarbonise industrial heat
investments.summary: Exergy3, a University of Edinburgh spinout, raised £10m in a seed round led by Axeleo Capital to commercialise its ultra-high-temperature thermal energy storage system. The technology converts surplus renewable electricity into industrial-grade heat up to 1,200°C, addressing both wasted renewable generation and the need for decarbonised industrial processes. The company piloted its system at Annandale Distillery, enabling one of the world's first low-carbon whisky productions.
investments.raw_snippet: Exergy3, a University of Edinburgh spinout founded on technology invented by Dr Adam Robinson, secured £10m in seed funding led by Axeleo Capital's Article 9 Green Tech Industry I fund. Other investors include Bayern Capital, Kibo Invest, Scottish Enterprise, Zero Carbon Capital, and Old College Capital. The technology converts surplus renewable electricity into industrial heat and was piloted at Annandale Distillery. Announced 21 April 2026.

investors: Investment Fund for Scotland (IFS Maven Equity Finance)
investments.headline: Maven leads £2.6 million funding round in Esk
investments.summary: Edinburgh-based Esk, which delivers high-end live experiences for blue-chip brands including Netflix, Paramount, and BAFTA, secured £2.6m from Maven Capital Partners via its VCTs and the Investment Fund for Scotland. The funding will support business development, marketing, IP portfolio growth, and development of the company's proprietary hardware technology. Esk has completed over 550 shows globally.
investments.raw_snippet: Maven leads £2.6m investment in Esk, an Edinburgh-based global entertainment technology company that delivers high-end live experiences for brands such as Netflix, Paramount and BAFTA. Funding from Maven Income and Growth VCTs and the Investment Fund for Scotland (IFS Maven Equity Finance), delivered by the British Business Bank. The capital will enable Esk to invest in business development, marketing, IP portfolio expansion, and proprietary hardware development. Announced May 11, 2026.

investors: Investment Fund for Scotland (IFS Maven Equity Finance)
investments.lead_investor: Investment Fund for Scotland (IFS Maven Equity Finance)
investments.headline: IFS Maven Equity Finance invests £1.25 million in Highway Data Systems
investments.summary: Glasgow-based Highway Data Systems (HDS) secured £1.25m from the Investment Fund for Scotland, managed by Maven Capital Partners and delivered by the British Business Bank. HDS develops automated quality assurance technology combining hardware, electronics, and software to replace manual testing processes in road construction. HDS was the first business to secure funding through the Greater Glasgow Innovation Cluster.
investments.raw_snippet: IFS Maven Equity Finance invests £1.25 million in Highway Data Systems, a Glasgow-based company that develops automated quality assurance technologies for the road construction and asphalt industries. HDS serves major contractors including Eurovia, Holcim, and Tarmac across UK and US markets. The company is the first to secure investment through the Greater Glasgow Innovation Cluster. Announced April 30, 2026.

investments.headline: GovTech startup ePass rolls out nationwide platform after £1m debut year
investments.summary: Edinburgh-based ePass, founded in 2024 by James Buchan and Chris Renga following a CivTech competition win, achieved £1m revenue in its first twelve months and deployed its licensing platform across all 32 Scottish local authorities serving over 15,000 businesses. The company is actively engaging with VC and investor communities and is likely to pursue its first external fundraising round later in 2026. This is not yet a closed investment round.
investments.raw_snippet: GovTech startup ePass generated £1m revenue in its debut year and rolled out its platform nationwide across Scotland's 32 local authorities. Co-founders James Buchan (CEO) and Chris Renga (CTO) note the company is 'actively engaging with venture capital and investor communities' and is 'likely to embark on its first external fundraising round later this year.' No external VC investment has yet been closed as of announcement date May 25, 2026.
